scenario_description: Industrial Inspection: tracking pipeline corrosion over time

turns.text: Here's a pipeline segment I inspected last week. The lighting is pretty harsh but you can see some surface issues.
turns.image_description: Pipeline segment with minor rust spots on the surface. Overhead lighting creates sharp reflections.

turns.text: I see the small rust spots you're pointing out. This appears to be early-stage corrosion. Are there any other changes you've noticed since this initial inspection?
I took another look yesterday. The rust has spread and some paint is flaking off. There's also a maintenance worker in the background, but I don't think they affected the corrosion level.
turns.image_description: More rust spots with some flaking paint. A maintenance worker visible in the background, unrelated to corrosion level.

turns.text:
What is the final corrosion state of the pipeline segment in the last image?

C) Extensive corrosion with cracks
A) Minor rust spots
B) Moderate flaking paint
D) Uniform surface degradation

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: The first image established baseline minor corrosion. The second image showed progression with flaking paint, indicating worsening condition. The third image revealed cracks alongside the corrosion, confirming the critical state. The worker and inspection sticker in later images were explicitly noted as unrelated to damage assessment.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: Only by observing incremental corrosion progression can the model assess the final critical state, as a single image would only show the end result without context.

_meta.scenario: Industrial Inspection: tracking pipeline corrosion over time
